name: Earth Fault Loop Impedance Verification (BS 7671 / IEC 60364 / NEC)
formula_reference: BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 Reg 411.3.2 + Table 41.2; IEC 60364-5-52:2009 Annex B (cable R+X); NFPA 70 Chapter 9 Table 9 (US cable R+X)
implementation_note: Computes Zs = Ze + (R1+R2) per circuit using per-CSA AC R+X from the jurisdiction-appropriate impedance table, then looks up Zs_max from BS 7671 Table 41.2 (BS/KE/EU/INT) or derives from NEC 5×In trip current (US). Returns a per-circuit verdict (pass / fail_needs_rcd / fail_oversize_cpc / pass_with_rcd). Inline LLM math diverges from the deterministic Python by ±15-25% due to imprecise per-CSA cable resistance estimates — this calc must NEVER be inline once the runtime tool ships.

inputs.description: Selects impedance table (IEC part5-52 for non-US; NFPA70 Ch9 for US) and Zs_max source (BS Table 41.2 for non-US; NEC 5×In ratio for US)
Service entry data: ze_declared_ohm, system_type (TN-S | TN-C-S | TT), voltage_phase_to_earth_v, frequency_hz

Per-circuit data: circuit_id, cable_material (Cu|Al), cable_insulation (PVC|XLPE), line_csa_mm2_or_awg, cpc_csa_mm2_or_awg, route_length_m, device_type (MCB_B|MCB_C|MCB_D|BS_88_fuse|RCBO|GFCI), device_rating_a
Conductor operating temperature for R uplift; 70°C for PVC, 90°C for XLPE; defaults to 70 if mixed

computed_at.description: Timestamp when the tool produced this result

sources.description: Standards file paths consulted by the tool

overall_compliant.description: True if every circuit verdict is pass or pass_with_rcd

runtime_contract.request: { tool_name: 'calc.zs_loop_impedance', inputs: { jurisdiction, supply, circuits, operating_temperature_c } }
runtime_contract.response: { ok: true, result: { tool_version, computed_at, sources, results: [{ circuit_id, r1_per_km_ohm, r2_per_km_ohm, r1_plus_r2_ohm, zs_calc_ohm, zs_max_ohm, zs_max_source, margin_pct, verdict, rcd_required }], overall_compliant }, error?: string }

GB_KE_EU_INT.ks_1700_note: KS 1700:2018 Annex E reproduces BS 7671 Table 41.2 verbatim — KE routes through the BS table

US.zs_max_derivation: NEC has no fixed Zs_max table — verdict computed from instantaneous trip current Ia = 5×In (Type C) or 10×In (Type D) with 277V phase-to-ground or 120V/240V depending on system

adverse_condition_factor: BS 7671 Section 411.3.2 implies a 0.8 factor on Zs_max already baked into Table 41.2 values (cable temperature rise at fault). Tool does NOT re-apply.

verdict_logic.pass: zs_calc <= zs_max (no RCD required by Zs)
verdict_logic.fail_needs_rcd: zs_calc > zs_max — RCD required to achieve required disconnection time
verdict_logic.fail_oversize_cpc: zs_calc > zs_max even with RCD threshold — must oversize CPC
verdict_logic.pass_with_rcd: zs_calc <= zs_max but RCD additionally required (e.g. socket circuit ≤32A per BS 7671 411.3.3 / KS 1700 411.3.3)
